loreEntries.content: Madame Mvashti is one of Sandpoint's oldest residents, a Varisian seer whose reputation for genuine insight has outlasted the skepticism of every newcomer who dismissed her. She offers occult spellcasting and maintains a spare room for travelers, but those who come looking for a novelty fortune-teller tend to leave unsettled in ways they didn't anticipate.

loreEntries.title: The Old Light
loreEntries.summary: Sandpoint's ancient ruined lighthouse — a Thassilonian remnant that Brodert Quink insists was once a weapon capable of destroying anything within a mile.
loreEntries.content: The Old Light is a ruined lighthouse that stretches above Sandpoint's coastal bluffs, a relic of the Thassilonian empire that predates the town by millennia. Most residents treat it as a landmark. Brodert Quink, the local Thassilonian scholar, has spent years arguing that it was never a lighthouse at all — that it was a war machine capable of projecting fire at range. Nobody believed him, for a long time. The return of New Thassilon and its Runelords has made people reconsider.

loreEntries.title: Brodert Quink
loreEntries.summary: Sandpoint's Thassilonian scholar — decades of study and no prestige to show for it, until recently when people started realizing he'd been right all along.
loreEntries.content: Brodert studied with dwarven engineers at Janderhoff and catalogued ruins at the Founder's Archive in Magnimar. He's spent years insisting the Old Light was a war machine. The town is only now catching up to him, and he has mixed feelings about being vindicated this way.

loreEntries.title: Chopper's Isle
loreEntries.summary: A rocky island just off the coast — where Sandpoint's most infamous killer lived, and where the ruins of his home still sit, untouched.
loreEntries.content: Chopper's Isle is the small rocky outcrop where Jervis Stoot, the serial killer known as Chopper, carved his grotesque menagerie of birds into every available surface and eventually killed over two dozen Sandpoint residents before being stopped. Nobody has demolished the carvings or the ruins of his home. Nobody goes there if they can help it. Children dare each other to swim out to it.

loreEntries.title: Junker's Edge
loreEntries.summary: The cliff at the edge of town where Sandpoint disposes of its refuse — and where Gorvi, the town's half-orc dung-sweeper, holds quiet authority.
loreEntries.content: Junker's Edge is the waste disposal point for most of Sandpoint's refuse, a stretch of cliff from which garbage is dumped into the sea below. It is overseen by Gorvi, the town's half-orc dung-sweeper, who employs a small crew of youths and lives in a shack on Tower Street by choice rather than necessity.

loreEntries.title: Gorvi
loreEntries.summary: Sandpoint's dung-sweeper — one of the town's few half-orcs, paid well enough to live better than he does, and seemingly content with his shack and his crew.
loreEntries.content: Gorvi employs over two dozen youths to help manage Sandpoint's waste. He lives in a small, run-down shack despite earning a comfortable wage, and nobody has yet worked out whether that's philosophy or stubbornness.

loreEntries.title: Sandpoint General Store
loreEntries.summary: The Vinder family's general store — solid stock, a proprietor drinking himself apart in the cellar, and a wife holding things together by sheer will.
loreEntries.content: Sandpoint's general store is owned by Ven Vinder, who once took great pride in his business and his family. He's been spending most of his time in a cellar drinking hole since the murder of his eldest daughter. His wife Solsta runs the actual store and is increasingly worried about both her husband and their surviving daughter Shayliss, who has been coming home less and less.

loreEntries.title: Ven Vinder
loreEntries.summary: Owner of Sandpoint's general store — a father who lost a daughter to a serial killer and has been losing himself to grief ever since.
loreEntries.content: Ven used to be one of those men who measured their worth in their family. Now he measures it in how many hours he can spend in his cellar drinking before anyone comes looking for him.

loreEntries.title: Solsta Vinder
loreEntries.summary: Ven Vinder's wife — keeping the general store running, keeping the family together, and quietly terrified that she's losing both her husband and her daughter.
loreEntries.content: Solsta manages the store, manages her husband's deterioration, and is starting to manage her fear about where Shayliss keeps disappearing to. She has promised herself she'll ask the next group of capable-looking strangers for help.

loreEntries.title: Shayliss Vinder
loreEntries.summary: The Vinders' surviving daughter — she's found Calistria and a secret identity, and she's not coming home much anymore.
loreEntries.content: Shayliss has become someone her parents don't quite recognize. She's found faith in Calistria and an outlet in a masked vigilante identity called the Shroud. Whatever she's doing when she slips out of the secret door by the Turandarok River, it isn't what her mother imagines.

loreEntries.title: Rovanky Tannery
loreEntries.summary: Sandpoint's tannery — excellent leather goods, long waits, and a perfectionist owner who keeps firing his help for not meeting his standards.
loreEntries.content: Rovanky Tannery sits at the edge of town, as tanneries do. Larz Rovanky runs it with a demanding efficiency that results in exceptional products and a perpetually thin staff, since Larz regularly fires workers who don't meet his standards and ends up doing most of the work himself.

loreEntries.title: Larz Rovanky
loreEntries.summary: Owner of the Rovanky Tannery — a perfectionist who produces excellent leather goods, keeps firing his employees, and sees nothing contradictory about this.
loreEntries.content: Larz expects perfection. He has never met an employee who meets that standard for long. The leather goods are worth the wait. The wait is sometimes very long.

loreEntries.title: Deverin Manor
loreEntries.summary: Home of Sandpoint's mayor and the loud, sprawling Deverin family — perpetually crowded, perpetually political.
loreEntries.content: Deverin Manor houses Kendra Deverin alongside her brother's large and rambunctious family. Her sister-in-law Vana is a persistent presence who believes the family deserves more space, more luxury, and more acknowledgment than it currently receives. Kendra has been managing this with commendable patience.

loreEntries.title: Scarnetti Manor
loreEntries.summary: The Scarnetti family estate — recently rebuilt after giant-raid damage, and paid for with money from a source Titus would rather not discuss.
loreEntries.content: Scarnetti Manor was damaged during a giant raid and spent several years in disrepair while the family's reputation suffered. Titus Scarnetti has since rebuilt both, partly through a deal with Korvosan benefactors whose interest in Sandpoint's affairs he reports on seasonally. The manor is restored. The questions about who paid for it haven't gone away.

loreEntries.title: Kaijitsu Manor
loreEntries.summary: The Kaijitsu family estate — largely empty now, with its most famous resident an empress on the other side of the world.
loreEntries.content: Kaijitsu Manor stands as a monument to a family that made Sandpoint's glasswork famous and then departed for a far grander stage. With Ameiko Kaijitsu now Empress of Minkai, the manor is maintained but quiet, and the glassworks it once supported stand closed and rumored-haunted nearby.

loreEntries.title: Valdemar Manor
loreEntries.summary: The former seat of the Valdemar family — largely vacated now, after Sandpoint's fourth founding family drifted away.
loreEntries.content: The Valdemars were one of Sandpoint's four founding families, their fortune built on shipbuilding and carpentry. The family has largely moved on from Sandpoint, and their manor reflects this departure — maintained but no longer a center of power. Their old seat on the town council sits vacant.

loreEntries.title: Scarnetti Mill
loreEntries.summary: The Scarnetti family's grain mill — one of their primary commercial operations, run by a man paid barely enough to stay and threatened enough not to leave.
loreEntries.content: Scarnetti Mill is one of the family's foundational commercial assets, managed by the perpetually anxious Courrin Whesterwill under conditions that might charitably be described as exploitative. Courrin stays because the alternative, as Titus has made clear, involves unpleasant consequences for people Courrin cares about.

loreEntries.title: Goblin Squash Stables
loreEntries.summary: Sandpoint's stables — named with the particular enthusiasm of a man who really, really hates goblins.
loreEntries.content: Sandpoint's main stabling facility is owned by Daviren Hosk, who named it with characteristic restraint. It functions as a fully operational stable and is well-maintained. Daviren's ear collection is displayed near the entrance. Visitors are encouraged not to comment on it.

loreEntries.title: Sandpoint Shipyard
loreEntries.summary: Where Sandpoint's boats get built and repaired — a working industrial facility tied historically to the Valdemar family legacy.
loreEntries.content: The Sandpoint Shipyard is one of the town's major employers and a legacy of the Valdemar family's original investment in the settlement. With the Valdemars largely departed, the shipyard operates independently, building and repairing vessels for the fishing fleet and occasional larger commissions.

loreEntries.title: Hayliss Korvaski
loreEntries.summary: Jasper's sister and fellow Abadar devotee — without her brother's restraint, and not at all sorry about it.
loreEntries.content: Hayliss runs her shop with the same faith her brother Jasper holds, and none of his diplomatic temperament. She charges Scarnetti family members extra and considers the mayor's requests to keep the peace to be advisory at best. She has recently seen something unusual involving the Vinder girl, and she's looking for someone discreet to investigate.
